title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions Object
description: A collection of BRepWireEdgeDefinition objects. Using this collection you can create new BRepWireDefinition objects to full define a wire body.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new BRepWireEdgeDefinition object associated with the parent BRepWireDefinition object.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified BRepWireEdgeDefinition object using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of B-Rep wire edge definition objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: BRep Body definition Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating BRep bodies by BRepBodyDefinition.

add.title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions.add Method
add.description: Creates a new BRepWireEdgeDefinition object associated with the parent BRepWireDefinition object.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created BRepWireEdgeDefinition object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: startVertex
parameters.description: Vertex definition that defines the start of the edge. For a closed curve, like a circle, you still need to provide a vertex on the curve but should use the same BRepVertexDefinition for both the start and end vertices.

parameters.name: endVertex
parameters.description: Vertex definition that defines the end of the edge. For a closed curve, like a circle, this should be the same vertex as used for the start vertex.

parameters.name: modelSpaceCurve
parameters.description: A Curve3D object that defines the shape of the edge using 3D geometry in model space. Valid input is an Arc3D, NurbsCurve3D, Circle3D, Ellipse3D, EllipticalArc3D, or Line3D.

related_samples.name: BRep Body definition Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating BRep bodies by BRepBodyDefinition.

classType.title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified BRepWireEdgeDefinition object using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions.count Property
count.description: The number of B-Rep wire edge definition objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bRepWireEdgeDefinitions_var.count

isValid.title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bRepWireEdgeDefinitions_var.isValid

objectType.title: BRepWireEdgeDefinitions.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bRepWireEdgeDefinitions_var.objectType
